'''David Mycoe''' (born 1 May 1972) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s and 2000s. He played at club level for [[Sheffield Eagles (1984)|Sheffield Eagles]], [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]], [[Dewsbury Rams]], [[Newcastle Thunder|Gateshead Thunder]] and [[Hunslet R.L.F.C.|Hunslet Hawks]], as a {{rlp|so}}.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Playing career==
Mycoe made his début for Sheffield Eagles in the [[1989–90 Rugby Football League season|1989–90 season]], aged 17. He played {{rlp|so}}, and scored 2-[[Goal (sport)#Rugby football|goal]]s in Sheffield Eagles' 16-29 defeat by [[Wakefield Trinity]] in the [[1992 Yorkshire Cup]] Final during the [[1992–93 Rugby Football League season|1992–93 season]] at [[Elland Road]], [[Leeds]] on Sunday 18 October 1992.

David was inducted into Sheffield Eagles Hall of Fame on 19 September 2025.
